title: Phanera stipularis
taxonomy: Phanera stipularis is classified in the family Euphorbiaceae. It belongs to the genus Phanera. This plant is further categorized as a species within the Euphorbiaceae family. Phanera stipularis is native to Southeast Asia and is often found in tropical forests. Its classification places it among the diverse and widespread Euphorbiaceae family.
uses: Phanera stipularis, also known as the golden rain tree, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat conditions such as fever and arthritis. Its leaves and bark are used to make herbal teas that are believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Traditional practitioners also use Phanera stipularis to treat skin conditions and wounds. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Phanera stipularis for medicinal purposes.

intro: Phanera stipularis, also known as the climbing milk vine, is a tropical plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae, characterized by its climbing habit and unique, milky latex. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help to alleviate conditions such as fever, wounds, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Phanera stipularis include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Phanera stipularis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and sore throat.
